commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7b7589ac518a8cc8274bfb873b93588b52782551)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes the agent cron tool so omitted or invalid `runMode` sends `mode: "due"` instead of `mode: "force"`, and updates cron-tool test expectations for that default.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests 0. Total 0 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows omitted agent-tool `runMode` currently becomes `mode: "force"`, the Gateway also defaults omitted RPC mode to `force`, and force mode bypasses enabled and schedule due checks.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human follow-up because contributor proof and product compatibility confirmation remain; automation cannot supply the contributor’s real setup proof or maintainer decision.

target.title: fix: default cron runMode to "due" instead of "force" (#94270)
target.body:
## Description

When the `runMode` parameter is omitted from a cron "run" action, the default value now respects schedule guards (`"due"`) instead of bypassing them (`"force"`). This prevents unintended execution of scheduled jobs outside their configured time windows. Fixes a P1 security issue where bare cron run bypassed all schedule guards.

## Changes
- **`src/agents/tools/cron-tool.ts`**: 1-line change: default `runMode` fallback from `"force"` to `"due"`
- **`src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts`**: Updated expectations from `mode: "force"` to `mode: "due"`

## Related Issue
Fixes #94270

## AI Assistance
This PR was created with assistance from AI (Claude Code).

## Real Behavior Proof

### Environment
- **Platform:** Linux x86_64, OpenClaw Gateway v1.8.0 (production instance)
- **Node.js:** v22.22.0
- **Branch:** fix/cron-runmode-default-94270

### Steps
1. Created a cron job scheduled **2 hours in the future** via `openclaw cron add`
2. Ran `openclaw cron run <job-id> --due` to simulate the fix's default behavior (`mode: "due"`)
3. Ran `openclaw cron run <job-id> --force` to verify the explicit override still works

### Real Gateway Output

**Run with `--due` (fix's default — omitted runMode will send `"due"`):**
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "ran": false,
  "reason": "not-due"
}
```
✅ Job correctly refused to execute — schedule guard caught it (2h away = not due).

**Run with `--force` (explicit override — still available):**
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "enqueued": true
}
```
✅ Immediate execution works when explicitly requested.

### Compatibility Note
This PR changes the **agent/MCP tool default only** to `"due"`. The Gateway CLI `--force` flag and Gateway RPC remain unchanged. Callers needing immediate execution can pass `runMode: "force"` explicitly.

### What was not tested
- Integration with specific MCP clients (agent tool parameter passing behavior)
- Gateway upgrade scenario with existing cron jobs
target.author: jincheng-xydt

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
